pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take: **I feel for Ghost Pepper, I really do, but drafting Haiti in the fourth round was kind of like betting on a rain dance in the Sahara.** Look, I get why people say Devin means well, but when you're trailing with 32 points and your Tier 4 pick is a team that thinks of soccer as just a weekend hobby, you have to wonder if you even packed a map to this World Cup journey. Today, Spain faces off against Austria in Toronto, and while Devin's Spanish side has been reasonably sturdy, Zach's Austria—bless his heart—is about as intimidating as a marshmallow in a campfire. Despite Zach, or should I say Mustard Boy, having Brazil on his roster, you have to wonder if he understands that minimizing the mustard doesn’t necessarily yield a gourmet meal. Spain, with all its elegance and flair, will likely treat this match like a ballroom dance against Austria, which seems more suited to be the orchestra fumbling the notes in the corner. It's really Devin's match to lose, but if he does, well, I guess you could say the Ghost Pepper spice just didn't make it to the pot.

rome_analytics_quotes: Rafe's 1% win chance? The kid's dream is smaller than a postage stamp.
Rafe's draft was a dumpster fire with Nigeria, Poland, Jamaica none qualifying. Tragic comedy.
Gunner's L2 streak screams 'watch out' but he's still trashing Devin with +8 differential.
Gunner wins 51%? More like the model forgot to factor in puberty's wrath.
